summary: Excellent soundboard capture of a focused 6/14/92 show, highlighted by a gorgeous Desolation Row solo and a badass The Other One > Morning Dew sequence.
review: The performance is praised as mature and proficient, with Jerry Garcia’s delicate solo on Desolation Row and the fiery The Other One into Morning Dew standing out. The setlist skips the usual hits in favor of deeper cuts like Samson and Ship of Fools, and the encore trippy Baba O’Riley > Tomorrow Never Knows caps a solid night. The soundboard recording is reported as clear with no notable issues.

ai_rating.rationale: One enthusiastic 5‑star review highlights an outstanding show and reports a clean soundboard source; limited sample size keeps confidence at medium.

recording_quality.technical_notes: No complaints about clarity or mix; reviewer implies the recording is crisp and fully captures the performance.

show_quality.setlist_flow: A mature, risk‑taking set that forgoes the usual hits in favor of deeper catalog cuts and surprising pairings, delivering a focused and immersive second half.

band_member_comments.Jerry: His simple yet gorgeous solo on Desolation Row is singled out as a highlight.
band_member_comments.Keys: Steve Miller’s guest contributions are described as "just gravy" adding extra flavor to the show.
